audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim Song of Moses and Nebo-death notice, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for heaven/earth witness, Rock theology, textual variants concerning nations and holy beings, eagle and birth imagery, Jeshurun prosperity critique, demon/new-god polemic, Divine anger and vengeance, enemy taunt, restoration, final nations verse, Torah-as-life, and Moses' death boundary.

verses.restored: Give ear, heavens, and I will speak; let earth hear sayings of my mouth.
May my teaching drop as rain, my speech distill as dew, like showers upon tender grass and like abundant rain upon vegetation.
For name of YHWH I proclaim; ascribe greatness to our Divine.
The Rock—complete is his work, for all his ways are judgment; a Divine of faithfulness and without injustice, righteous and upright is he.
They have acted corruptly toward him; not his children—their blemish; a generation twisted and crooked.
Is this how you repay YHWH, foolish and unwise people? Is he not your father who acquired/made you? He made you and established you.
Remember days of old; understand years of generation and generation. Ask your father and he will tell you, your elders and they will say to you.
When Most High gave nations their inheritance, when he separated children of humanity, he set boundaries of peoples according to number of children of Israel/sons of Divine.
For portion of YHWH is his people; Jacob is lot of his inheritance.
He found him in wilderness land, in waste, howling desolation; he surrounded him, cared for him, guarded him like pupil of his eye.
Like eagle stirring its nest, hovering over its young, spreading its wings, taking them, bearing them upon its pinions,
YHWH alone led him, and no foreign god was with him.
He made him ride upon heights of earth and eat produce of field; he made him suck honey from crag and oil from flinty rock,
curds of cattle and milk of flock, with fat of lambs, rams of Bashan and goats, with finest kidney-fat of wheat; and blood of grape you drank as wine.
Jeshurun grew fat and kicked—you grew fat, thick, and sleek—and he abandoned Divine who made him and treated as foolish the Rock of his salvation.
They made him jealous with strangers; with abominations they provoked him.
They sacrificed to demons, not Divine, to gods they had not known, new ones recently come, whom your fathers did not fear.
The Rock who gave birth to you, you neglected, and you forgot Divine who brought you forth in labor.
YHWH saw and spurned, from provocation of his sons and his daughters.
He said, I will hide my face from them; I will see what their end will be, for a generation of reversals are they, children in whom is no faithfulness.
They made me jealous with a no-god; they provoked me with their vanities. So I will make them jealous with a no-people; with a foolish nation I will provoke them.
For fire is kindled in my anger and burns to lowest Sheol; it consumes earth and its produce and sets foundations of mountains aflame.
I will heap evils upon them; my arrows I will spend against them.
Wasted by hunger, consumed by burning plague and bitter destruction, I will send teeth of beasts against them, with venom of crawlers of dust.
Outside sword shall bereave, and inside chambers terror—young man and virgin, nursing child with gray-haired one.
I said, I will scatter them; I will cause memory of them to cease from humanity,
if not for provocation of enemy, lest their adversaries misjudge, lest they say, Our hand is exalted, and YHWH did not do all this.
For a nation perishing in counsel are they, and there is no understanding in them.
If only they were wise, they would understand this; they would discern their latter end.
How could one pursue a thousand and two put ten thousand to flight unless their Rock sold them and YHWH delivered them?
For their rock is not like our Rock, and our enemies are judges.
For their vine is from vine of Sodom and from fields of Gomorrah; their grapes are grapes of poison, bitter clusters are theirs.
Their wine is venom of serpents and cruel poison of cobras.
Is it not stored up with me, sealed in my treasuries?
Vengeance and repayment are mine, for time when their foot slips; for day of their calamity is near, and what is prepared for them hastens.
For YHWH will judge his people and have compassion/relent concerning his servants, when he sees that power is gone and there is none remaining, restrained or released.
He will say, Where are their gods, rock in which they sought refuge,
who ate fat of their sacrifices and drank wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise and help you; let there be shelter over you.
See now that I, I am he, and there is no god with me. I put to death and I make alive; I wound and I heal, and none can deliver from my hand.
For I lift my hand to heavens and say, As I live forever,
if I sharpen lightning of my sword and my hand takes hold on judgment, I will return vengeance to my adversaries and repay those hating me.
I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall eat flesh—from blood of slain and captives, from heads of leaders of enemy.
Rejoice, nations, with his people/praise his people, nations, for he avenges blood of his servants and returns vengeance to his adversaries, and makes atonement/covering for his land and his people.
Moses came and spoke all words of this song in ears of people, he and Joshua/Hoshea son of Nun.
Moses finished speaking all these words to all Israel.
He said to them, Set your heart to all words by which I testify against you today, which you shall command your children to keep to do, all words of this Torah/instruction.
For it is not an empty word for you, for it is your life; and by this word you shall lengthen days upon ground where you are crossing Jordan to possess it.
YHWH spoke to Moses on that same day, saying,
Go up this mountain of Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in land of Moab opposite Jericho, and see land of Canaan that I give to children of Israel as possession.
Die on mountain where you go up and be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his people,
because you broke faith with me in midst of children of Israel at waters of Meribah-kadesh, wilderness of Zin, because you did not sanctify me in midst of children of Israel.
For from opposite you shall see land, but there you shall not enter, into land I give to children of Israel.

translation_notes: Verse 8 has a major textual variant: the Masoretic tradition reads 'children of Israel,' while Dead Sea Scroll and Septuagintal witnesses support 'sons of God/divine beings.' The restored line preserves both for audit.
Verse 43 also varies substantially across textual witnesses, especially regarding nations, servants, land, and people; no single harmonized form should hide that history.
The Rock metaphor combines stability, generation, judgment, and salvation; verse 18 also uses maternal birth imagery for Divine creation of Israel.
Jeshurun's growing fat is a metaphor for prosperity producing complacency and must not be used for body shaming.
Demon and new-god polemic belongs to Israel's covenant song and must not justify persecution of other religions or spiritual practices.

The song contains severe Divine violence, collective punishment, famine, plague, terror, and blood-soaked vengeance imagery. It must not authorize human violence or explain modern suffering as deserved.
'Vengeance is mine' may restrain private revenge, but the surrounding Divine-war rhetoric still requires ethical protest and non-reenactment.
The Song is a major Jewish liturgical and interpretive text, known as Ha'azinu, and must not be appropriated through supersessionist readings.
Torah is named not an empty word but Israel's life; this affirmation directly resists contempt for Jewish law as dead legalism.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 32 to justify war, genocide, plague theology, religious persecution, antisemitism, supersessionism, body shaming, or revenge. Preserve the poetry, textual plurality, maternal and protective Divine imagery, and Torah-as-life affirmation while receiving its catastrophe and vengeance language under categorical non-reenactment and moral protest.
